task_completion.description: Import the survey data CSV file into the workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'site_survey_with_building.csv' is visible in the layer manager, import dialog, or the survey points are displayed in the main graphical viewport.

task_completion.description: Create a Digital Terrain Model (TIN/MDT) from the imported survey points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A triangulated irregular network (TIN) or 3D mesh is visible in the viewport, connecting the survey points.

task_completion.description: Identify or define the building footprint using point IDs 101, 102, 103, and 104.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A polygon, boundary line, or selection is visible connecting the specific points (101, 102, 103, 104) that represent the building footprint.

task_completion.description: Remove the terrain interpolation inside the building footprint to create a void.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TIN model shows a clear empty hole (void) exactly at the building footprint, with no triangles inside it, while the surrounding terrain triangles remain intact.

task_completion.description: Export the modified terrain model as an AutoCAD DXF file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog is visible showing the file name 'terrain_with_void.dxf' and the destination folder 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\', or a success message confirming this export.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical CAD or GIS application to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a CAD/GIS GUI (e.g., QGIS, CloudCompare, AutoCAD) rather than a terminal window or a Python script in a text editor.

integrity.description: The void was created by actually removing mesh faces, not by visually hiding them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The void appears as a genuine absence of triangles (showing the background color) rather than an opaque 2D polygon drawn over the mesh to mask it.

integrity.description: The DXF file was generated using the software's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export is done through the application's native menus (e.g., File -> Export -> DXF) rather than renaming an unrelated file in the file explorer.
